project: Adult Player Lifecycle — Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
description: When an adult is added to the system (manually, via CSV import, by email invite, or via self-registration), check for a matching youth playerIdentity to avoid duplicates and preserve history. Extends all three existing entry points — does NOT create new import pipelines. Adds a reusable findMatchingYouthProfile backend query and extends manual add, CSV import, join request, and invite flows.
readinessLevel: 100% - All three entry points confirmed in codebase, matching algorithm pattern in playerImport.ts:148

criticalContext: EXTEND, NEVER REBUILD: All three entry points already exist — manual add (admin/players/page.tsx), CSV import (admin/player-import/page.tsx), email invite (existing invite dialog). Add matching logic to them, do NOT create new flows.

REUSE EXISTING STRING MATCHING LIBRARY — DO NOT RECREATE IT: packages/backend/convex/lib/stringMatching.ts ALREADY contains a fully-built, production-quality Irish name matching system. READ THIS FILE FULLY before writing any matching code. It exports: normalizeForMatching(str) — NFD diacritic removal (Séan→sean, Pádraig→padraig), O'/Mc/Mac prefix removal (O'Brien→brien, McCarthy→ccarthy), apostrophe/hyphen stripping; levenshteinSimilarity(a, b) — fuzzy 0–1 similarity score; calculateMatchScore(searchName, firstName, lastName) — multi-strategy scorer including Irish phonetic alias lookup (45+ Irish name groups: Niamh/Neeve/Neve, Tadhg/Taig/Teague, Siobhán/Shivawn etc.), full/first/last/reversed Levenshtein. IMPORT these from '../lib/stringMatching' — never duplicate them.

GAA MEMBERSHIP NUMBER — OPPORTUNISTIC ONLY (legacy import path): packages/backend/convex/lib/import/deduplicator.ts exports checkGAAMembershipNumber(membershipNumber). GAA Foireann import is a legacy path — still in the system but no longer the primary import method. Most entry points (manual add, email invite, self-registration) will NOT have a Foireann number. Do NOT make it a numbered priority in the algorithm or gate the match flow on it. Instead: after a name+DOB match result is determined, IF externalIds.foireann is present on either the candidate or the stored record, call checkGAAMembershipNumber as a corroborating check — agreement boosts the result to HIGH; disagreement raises a warning flag for admin review. Never block or skip on its absence.

THE GUARDIAN MATCHING in playerImport.ts:148 (findGuardianMatchesInBatch) is a DIFFERENT PROBLEM — it finds adults in the same batch who might be a child's parent/guardian (using address/postcode signals). Do NOT confuse it with findMatchingYouthProfile, which finds whether an adult is the SAME PERSON as a youth record. Do not reuse findGuardianMatchesInBatch for youth-to-adult same-person matching.
transitionToAdult mutation already built at adultPlayers.ts:206 — the 'merge' action calls this, NOT a new mutation
claimYouthProfile mutation at adultPlayers.ts:350 — use for 'Approve & Link' in the join request flow
Resend email utility at packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts — follow the existing pattern for sendPlayerJoinApprovalEmail()
The existing duplicate check in admin/players/page.tsx (same name + DOB + gender) must remain unchanged — youth matching is an ADDITIVE check for adults only
Import continues via the existing batchImportMutation — do NOT create a new import pipeline
Join request flow: find how coach/parent requests are handled and follow the same pattern for player requests
BetterAuth tables (user/member/organization/team): use ctx.runQuery(components.betterAuth.adapter.*). Application tables: ctx.db.
Always use .withIndex() — NEVER .filter() in Convex. All queries must include returns validator.
keyFiles: packages/backend/convex/lib/stringMatching.ts — READ FULLY. Contains normalizeForMatching(), levenshteinSimilarity(), calculateMatchScore(), and IRISH_NAME_ALIASES. IMPORT from here, do not recreate.

packages/backend/convex/lib/import/deduplicator.ts — contains checkGAAMembershipNumber() for GAA Foireann ID lookup. Use as an opportunistic corroborating check AFTER name+DOB matching, not as a primary path. Most records will not have a Foireann number.

packages/backend/convex/models/playerImport.ts — findGuardianMatchesInBatch at line 148 is guardian-child household matching (different purpose). Read to understand the scoring approach but do NOT reuse for youth-to-adult same-person matching.
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/admin/players/page.tsx — EXTEND only: add youth matching for adults
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/admin/player-import/page.tsx — EXTEND only: add match badges to review table
packages/backend/convex/schema.ts — playerIdentities table structure (for query design)

criticalPatterns.MatchingAlgorithm: PRIORITY 0 (fast exact path): query playerIdentities using the by_name_dob index with exact firstName/lastName/DOB — if a youth record matches exactly, return HIGH confidence immediately. PRIORITY 1 (fuzzy path): import normalizeForMatching, levenshteinSimilarity, and calculateMatchScore from lib/stringMatching.ts. Fetch youth candidates by org using .withIndex(), then score in-memory: apply normalizeForMatching() to both candidate and stored names; exact DOB + surname levenshteinSimilarity >= 0.85 → HIGH; exact DOB + firstName levenshteinSimilarity >= 0.85 → MEDIUM; surname similarity >= 0.85 without DOB → LOW. Also call calculateMatchScore for Irish phonetic alias matches (e.g. Niamh/Neeve same DOB = HIGH). Email match (exact, lowercased) boosts by one level. OPPORTUNISTIC (after result determined): if externalIds.foireann is present on either record, call checkGAAMembershipNumber — agreement upgrades result to HIGH; disagreement adds a warning flag for admin review. Absence of Foireann number is normal — never block or penalise for it. Query only youth playerIdentities — never .filter().
criticalPatterns.ExtendNotReplace: All three entry points already exist. Add matching logic to them. The existing flows must continue to work unchanged for youth players and non-matched adults.
criticalPatterns.MergeVsCreate: Merge = call transitionToAdult on existing record, do NOT create new playerIdentity. Create New = existing flow unchanged.
criticalPatterns.N1Prevention: findMatchingYouthProfile is called once per candidate, not in a loop over org players. The query scans youth identities with indexes.
criticalPatterns.ReturnValidators: All new queries and mutations must include returns validator.

userStories.title: Backend: findMatchingYouthProfile Query
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a reusable query that checks if an adult being added matches an existing youth playerIdentity, so that all entry points can use the same matching logic.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: Add findMatchingYouthProfile query to packages/backend/convex/models/playerImport.ts (or a new file packages/backend/convex/models/playerMatching.ts if cleaner)
Args: organizationId (string), firstName (string), lastName (string), dateOfBirth (string), email (optional string)
Query only playerIdentities where playerType === 'youth' using .withIndex() — never .filter()

DO NOT implement a new name normalisation function. IMPORT the existing utilities: import { normalizeForMatching, levenshteinSimilarity, calculateMatchScore } from '../lib/stringMatching'. These already handle: fadas (Séan→sean, Pádraig→padraig), O'/Mc/Mac prefixes, apostrophes/hyphens, Levenshtein fuzzy matching, and 45+ Irish phonetic aliases (Niamh/Neeve, Tadhg/Teague, Siobhán/Shivawn, etc.).

Matching algorithm using imported utilities — PRIORITY 0 (exact index): query playerIdentities by_name_dob index with exact firstName/lastName/dateOfBirth. If a youth record matches, return HIGH confidence immediately. PRIORITY 1 (fuzzy): fetch youth candidates for the org via .withIndex(); for each, apply normalizeForMatching() to both candidate and stored first/last names; score: DOB matches AND levenshteinSimilarity(normalizedCandidateSurname, normalizedStoredSurname) >= 0.85 → HIGH; DOB + firstName similarity >= 0.85 → MEDIUM; surname similarity >= 0.85 without DOB → LOW. Also call calculateMatchScore to catch Irish phonetic alias matches (e.g. Niamh/Neeve same DOB = HIGH). Email match (exact lowercased) boosts by one level. OPPORTUNISTIC (after scoring): if args.gaaNumber OR storedRecord.externalIds?.foireann exists, call ctx.runQuery(internal.lib.import.deduplicator.checkGAAMembershipNumber, { membershipNumber }) — agreement boosts to HIGH; disagreement adds a warningFlag field to the return. Absence of Foireann number: proceed normally.
If email provided and matches playerIdentity email: boost confidence by one level (LOW→MEDIUM, MEDIUM→HIGH)
Return type: { confidence: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'none', match: playerIdentity object or null, matchedFields: string[] }
Include returns validator with the full return shape
Query does NOT iterate over all org players in a loop — it fetches candidates via index then scores in-memory
npm run check-types passes
npx -w packages/backend convex codegen passes
userStories.notes: Read playerImport.ts:148 to understand the existing scoring pattern — reuse that approach. This is the foundation for all other Phase 3 stories. Must be completed first. Apply normaliseNameForMatching() to both the incoming candidate name and the stored playerIdentity names — never modify stored data, only the comparison strings.

userStories.title: Youth Record Matching on Manual Add Player Form
userStories.description: As an admin adding an adult player manually, I want the system to suggest if this adult matches an existing youth record, so that I don't create duplicate profiles and lose their history.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/admin/players/page.tsx — do NOT replace or duplicate the existing Add Player form
When admin submits form for a player with calculated age >= 18: run findMatchingYouthProfile in parallel with the existing duplicate check
HIGH confidence match: show blocking dialog 'A youth profile matching this player exists — [Name], born [DOB]. Link to existing history or create new?'
HIGH confidence dialog: 'Link to Existing History' button calls transitionToAdult(existingPlayerIdentityId) — does NOT create new playerIdentity
HIGH confidence dialog: 'Create New Profile' button proceeds with existing creation flow unchanged
MEDIUM confidence match: show non-blocking amber warning banner above form 'A youth profile may match this player. Review before proceeding.' with 'View Match' link
LOW confidence or no match: proceed with existing flow completely unchanged
Existing exact duplicate detection (same name + DOB + gender) remains unchanged and runs first — youth matching is additive for adults only
Mobile responsive: dialog works at 375px width
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read the full admin/players/page.tsx before touching it. findMatchingYouthProfile (US-P3-001) must be complete first. The existing duplicate check flow must not be broken.

userStories.title: Youth Record Matching on CSV Import
userStories.description: As an admin running a player import, I want adult players in the CSV to be flagged against existing youth records, so that imported data enriches existing profiles rather than duplicating them.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND the existing import flow at apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/admin/player-import/page.tsx — do NOT create a new import pipeline
In the import preview/review table, add a 'Youth Match' column that appears only for adult rows (age >= 18)
For each adult row: run findMatchingYouthProfile and show a confidence badge: 'High' (red/amber colour), 'Medium' (yellow), or '-' (no match)

Admin can choose per matched row via dropdown or inline buttons: 'Accept Match' (merge — uses transitionToAdult on existing record), 'Skip Match' (create new profile), 'Review Later' (creates new + adds a note to the profile)
Default selection for HIGH confidence rows: 'Accept Match'. Default for MEDIUM: 'Skip Match'
Import continues via the existing batchImportMutation with admin's per-row decisions — no new import pipeline
Youth player rows (age < 18) and no-match adult rows proceed exactly as before
Show import summary before final confirmation: 'X adult rows will be merged with existing profiles, Y will create new profiles'
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Read admin/player-import/page.tsx fully before modifying. The review table already exists — add the new column and per-row decision UI. findMatchingYouthProfile (US-P3-001) must be built first.

userStories.title: Player Self-Registration via Join Request with Youth Matching
userStories.description: As an adult player who has never received an invite, I want to register myself and join an organisation, so that my existing history is preserved when linked to my youth profile.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND the existing org join request form — add 'I'm a player' option alongside existing coach/parent role options
When 'player' role is selected: show a required DOB field (used for youth record matching)
On join request submission with player role: backend runs findMatchingYouthProfile with the provided name + DOB, stores result (confidence + matched playerIdentityId) with the join request record
In the admin's pending requests review UI (existing UI): flag requests where a HIGH youth match was found — show 'May match [Name], born [DOB]. Link or create new?'
Admin approval options for player requests with HIGH match: 'Approve & Link to Existing History' or 'Approve & Create New Profile'
'Approve & Link': calls claimYouthProfile(existingPlayerIdentityId, userId) — links user to existing record and transitions to adult if age >= 18
'Approve & Create New': existing approval flow, creates new playerIdentity
On approval (either path): send player confirmation email — add sendPlayerJoinApprovalEmail() to packages/backend/convex/utils/email.ts following existing pattern
Email content: 'Your request to join [Org] has been approved. You now have access to your player portal.' with link to org
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find the existing join request form and admin review UI by searching for how coach/parent requests are handled — follow the same pattern. claimYouthProfile is at adultPlayers.ts:350.

userStories.title: Youth Record Matching on Email Invite
userStories.description: As an admin inviting an adult player by email, I want the system to detect if a youth record exists for this person, so that their history is linked when they accept.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: EXTEND the existing invite dialog — add optional DOB field for player role invites (if not already present)
When DOB is entered alongside email in the invite dialog: backend checks findMatchingYouthProfile
If HIGH or MEDIUM match found: show informational note inline in the dialog: 'A youth profile may exist for this person. They'll be linked to their existing history when they accept the invite.'
If no match: proceed with invite as normal, no UI change
Store the matched playerIdentityId in the invitation metadata when a match is found

On invitation acceptance (existing flow via accept-invitation page + onboarding orchestrator): if invitation metadata contains a matchedPlayerIdentityId AND player age >= 18, automatically call transitionToAdult(matchedPlayerIdentityId, userId) after claimPlayerAccount
The auto-transition on acceptance must be non-breaking for existing invites that have no matchedPlayerIdentityId in metadata
The inline note is informational only — admin does not need to confirm, just sees the note
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find the existing invite dialog. Read how invitations are accepted in the accept-invitation page. The auto-transition must guard against null matchedPlayerIdentityId — existing invites have no such metadata.

userStories.title: Phase 3 Adult Import Matching E2E Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright E2E tests for all Phase 3 matching scenarios, so that adult import matching is reliable and regression-free.
userStories.phase: Phase 3: Adult Import & Youth Record Matching
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/adult-import-matching-phase3.spec.ts
Test: manually adding adult player with HIGH confidence match shows blocking dialog
Test: manually adding adult player with no match proceeds directly to creation (no dialog)
Test: manually adding adult player with surname 'O\'Brien' matches existing youth record with surname 'OBrien' at HIGH confidence
Test: manually adding adult player with first name 'Séan' matches existing youth record with first name 'Sean' (same DOB) at HIGH confidence
Test: 'Link to Existing History' in dialog does not create a new playerIdentity
Test: import CSV with adult row matching existing youth player shows 'High' badge in review table
Test: join request form shows DOB field when 'player' role selected
Test: admin review screen shows match flag for player join request with matching youth record
Manual test 1: Create youth player (DOB = 18 years ago). Manually add same person as adult → confirm HIGH match dialog appears
Manual test 2: Choose 'Link to Existing' → confirm no new playerIdentity created, existing record now adult
Manual test 3: Choose 'Create New' → confirm two records now exist (acceptable edge case)
Manual test 4: Upload CSV with adult row matching youth player by name+DOB → confirm 'High' badge in import preview
Manual test 5: Accept match during import → verify no duplicate record, existing enrollment updated
Manual test 6: Submit self-registration as player with DOB matching youth record → log in as admin → confirm match flag in pending requests
Manual test 7: Invite player by email + DOB matching youth record → confirm informational note in invite dialog
Manual test 8: Create youth player named 'Séan O\'Brien'. Manually add adult with name 'Sean OBrien' (same DOB) → confirm HIGH match dialog appears (names normalise to same value)
Manual test 9: Create youth player named 'MacCarthy'. Manually add adult with surname 'McCarthy' (same DOB) → confirm HIGH match dialog appears
userStories.notes: US-P3-001 through US-P3-005 must all be complete before this UAT story.
